question: Under the Supreme Court's holding in Cox v. Sony, is an internet service provider contributorily liable merely because it knows some subscribers use its service to infringe copyrights?
What are the two ways the Court says a copyright owner can show the intent required for contributory copyright infringement in Cox v. Sony?
Why did the Court conclude that Cox did not induce its subscribers' copyright infringement?
Why did the Court conclude that Cox's internet service was not a service tailored to infringement?
How did the Court treat the Fourth Circuit's rule that supplying a product with knowledge it will be used to infringe can be enough for contributory infringement?
According to Cox v. Sony, what role does the DMCA safe-harbor provision play in the Court's analysis of contributory liability?
What is the legal standard in this case?
According to Cox v. Sony, what is the complete three-part test for contributory copyright infringement?
